name: Rich
what_it_does: Python library for rich text and beautiful formatting in the terminal, providing tables, syntax-highlighted code, progress bars, spinners, markdown rendering, and structured logging output.
use_cases: Rendering agent evaluation results as formatted tables with color-coded scores for human-readable terminal reports
Displaying real-time agent task progress with multi-column progress bars and status spinners in operator dashboards
Pretty-printing JSON, Python data structures, and tracebacks with syntax highlighting for agent debugging sessions
Building agent CLI operator interfaces with panels, rules, and markdown rendering for structured status output
Enhancing agent log output with Rich's logging handler for colorized, aligned, syntax-highlighted log display
not_for: Full-screen interactive TUI applications with keyboard navigation and focus management — use Textual (same author) instead
Non-interactive or headless environments where ANSI escape codes are not supported and will corrupt captured output
Minimal-dependency production containers where the library footprint and startup overhead matter
best_when: You want beautiful, feature-rich terminal output for agent operator tooling, evaluation reports, or developer-facing CLI tools with zero configuration.
avoid_when: Your output is captured, piped, or logged to a file where ANSI escape sequences are not stripped — always detect TTY or set TERM=dumb in non-interactive environments.

auth.notes: Local Python library, no auth required

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Rich's own error messages are clear; its Traceback rendering with local variables is best-in-class for Python debugging
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Rendering operations are stateless display side effects and do not affect application logic
known_agent_gotchas: Rich auto-detects TTY and disables color in non-interactive environments, but force_terminal=True overrides this and will send ANSI codes to log files and pipes
Rich markup uses [bold], [red] syntax that conflicts with content containing square brackets — always escape literal brackets with [[ in user-generated content
Console objects are not thread-safe by default — use Console(thread_safe=True) for multi-threaded agent workflows or output may interleave
Rich's Live display and Progress cannot be nested with print() — all output during a Live context must go through the Live object or rendering is corrupted
Rich Traceback install (rich.traceback.install()) affects all uncaught exceptions globally — in library code this can override the host application's exception handling

security_score_components.security_notes: No network layer; secure by default. Rich Traceback with show_locals=True will display all local variables in tracebacks — avoid in production as this can expose secrets.
